mission.title: apisix: bug: /status/ready endpoint always returns 503 in file-driven standalone mode
mission.description: bug: /status/ready endpoint always returns 503 in file-driven standalone mode. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify apisix troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your apisix deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n apisix -l app.kubernetes.io/name=apisix
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=apisix -n apisix --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review apisix configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant apisix configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n apisix -l app.kubernetes.io/name=apisix
kubectl get configmap -n apisix -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=apisix
```
### Current Behavior

When running APISIX 3.13.0 in file-driven standalone mode (deployment.role=data_plane, config_provider=yaml), the `/status/ready` health check endpoint always returns HTTP 503 with error "worker id: X has not received

steps.title: Apply the fix for bug: /status/ready endpoint always returns 503 in…
steps.description:
The status API would not work in standalone mode, this has been fixed.
```yaml
{"error":"worker id: 0 has not received configuration","status":"error"}
```

steps.title: Confirm bug: /status/ready endpoint always returns 503 in… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=apisix -n apisix --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n apisix --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: The status API would not work in standalone mode, this has been fixed.
codeSnippets: 2025/01/10 00:41:47 [warn] 33#33: *3 [lua] init.lua:1003: status_ready(): worker id: 0 has not received configuration, context: ngx.timer

# config.yaml
deployment:
  role: data_plane
  role_data_plane:
    config_provider: yaml
apisix:
  enable_admin: false

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with apisix installed or the issue environment reproducible.
